refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety, clarifying the performance requirement for teleoperation communication.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under controlled network conditions and verify p95 latency is ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation sessions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low processing delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency.
DP2: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees.
DP3: Implement edge processing to reduce data transmission delays.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on communication protocol optimization, which is logically decoupled from other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol changes impacting interoperability with other modules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network link performance and consider fallback DPs if network QoS degrades.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication and control logic.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time telemetry data to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands for teleoperation control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope but less focused on communication latency specifics.
Physical vehicle components have minimal direct control over communication latency.
Highest containment score as it directly manages teleoperation communication.
Safety services monitor but do not control communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes latency control and minimizes diffusion of responsibility.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link quality managed outside Teleoperation module.
Risk of interface mismatches with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and monitor network link performance jointly with NGHE Solution module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear latency optimization focus and no coupling issues.

diffusion_risks: Some risk of diffusion if network link quality issues arise outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has highest containment score and clear ownership of latency-related DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end including Teleoperation module and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control DP to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance coordination with Network Link management to ensure latency targets are met.
Document interface requirements explicitly for Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments meet requirements with clear ownership and verification paths; no immediate need to zigzag or revise.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link quality to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low processing delay.

revise.why: No significant issues detected requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement protocol optimizations effectively.
open_risks: Network link quality variability impacting latency.
Interface coordination risks between Teleoperation and other modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance test results under representative network conditions.
Interface compliance verification between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
